questions.text: The number of permutations of n distinct objects taken r at a time is:
questions.explanation: nPr = n!/((n-r)!) where n ≥ r. This gives the number of arrangements of r objects from n distinct objects.

questions.text: nCr = nC(n-r) for all non-negative integers n and r.
questions.explanation: This is a fundamental property of combinations: nCr = nC(n-r). Choosing r objects is equivalent to leaving out (n-r) objects.

questions.text: State the fundamental principle of counting.
questions.correctAnswer: If one event can occur in m ways and another in n ways, then both events together can occur in m×n ways.
questions.explanation: Fundamental principle of counting (multiplication rule): If event A can occur in m ways and event B in n ways, then (A and B) can occur in m×n ways.
